prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
The final line is drawn, the last page signed,  
A quiet breath, no burden left to bind.  
The clock has stopped, the scattered thoughts now rest,  
A hollow echo where a fevered mind once pressed.  

No more the margins brimming with despair,  
No more the late nights, breathless, year by year.  
The weight, once thick as stone, has lifted, light—  
Like autumn leaves taking flight.  

I hold the thing I built, rough, real, not dreamed,  
A quiet triumph, barely named.  
The world outside hums on, unchanged, yet I—  
New, still, and strangely, free.
